text: The laparoscopic view shows the right lower quadrant with visible adhesions from the omentum attached to the appendix. The appendix and terminal ileum are clearly identifiable in the surgical field.

The surgeon uses the Ligasure energy device to transect adhesions, revealing the ligament of Traves, the mesoappendix, and the base of the appendix. The ligament of Traves is carefully transected to expose the base of the appendix, while protecting the cecum and terminal ileum. The mesoappendix, containing the blood supply to the appendix, is then transected with an energy device to maintain hemostasis, fully exposing the base of the appendix for further steps.

The surgeon uses a laparoscopic vascular stapler to close and transect the base of the appendix, ensuring the ileocecal valve is protected. After transection, the cavity is irrigated and aspirated to confirm hemostasis and the integrity of the staple line. The appendix is then placed into an endocatch bag for removal.
